key_insight: A shape-memory alloy/polymer periodic rod inherits thermal hysteresis from NiTiCu, causing its acoustic band gaps and transmission to follow different heating and cooling paths.
engineering_relevance: This enables temperature- and history-dependent tuning of phononic stop bands in lightweight composite rods for vibration, filtering, and acoustic control.
method: Applied the standard transfer-matrix method to a 1D periodic rod with alternating NiTiCu and Parylene C segments.
Computed Bloch band structures for the infinite lattice and transmission spectra for finite rods.
Evaluated heating and cooling branches separately to capture hysteretic elastic-modulus changes across the martensitic transformation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports calculated band structures and transmission spectra that form closed hysteresis loops in temperature–frequency space, with gap positions also varying with filling fraction.
watch_item: Predictions rely on thin-rod, longitudinal-wave assumptions and accurate temperature-dependent material data, so implementation may be sensitive to geometry and phase-fraction modeling.
paper_title: Hysteretic Acoustic Band Structures in Shape-Memory Composite Thin Rods
